candidates.stmt: I came to the UK when I was 8 years old and this is now my home. I’ve lived in the area for decades and over the years I have volunteered with community groups and worked in local schools as a teaching assistant and as a teacher. London as a vibrant melting pot of different cultures inspires me, and I’m proud to stand with the Green Party of hope against those who aim to divide us through hate.

Vote Green for hope in a better future.

For a borough where residents are listened to.

Local policies for a greener, sustainable future.

Cleaner air and more affordable housing.

Repairs and improvements for residents, not demolitions for the benefit of billionaire investors.

An end to H&F's pension fund investing in the Israeli arms industry and all weapons factories.

I'm standing in Coningham because I want to make a difference in the community I know and love. I've worked at councils for 7 years so I know how they're run. I'm a local campaigner who will continue to work hard to represent residents and push H&F to go further and faster on green issues and social justice.

Hello! I’m Fatima & I would love to be your green party councillor. Being a friendly face where people can proudly say they know their councillor!

Growing up in H&F, I’m incredibly proud to be from here & around such amazing communities and local spaces. I know the local people, hardships people face and have seen the neglectful condition the Labour Council have left areas.

I’m tired of people saying this is ‘normal’, and expected to put up with this. I am committed to making Coningham work for those that live here, investing in our much loved local spaces and ensuring change supports the community, not pushes it aside.

Vote for change! Vote Green⭐️

I'm standing for the Green party in the Coningham ward of H&F, because I believe in community, sustainability and solidarity with the victims of genocide. On the climate and ecology crisis, the Labour administration talks a good game, but is not acting with anything like enough urgency. Nor is it doing enough to tackle social problems, from street crime to poorly maintained housing and local faciltities, and a run-down Uxbridge Road. I would work tirelessly to make a difference in the neighbourhood I've called home for 26 years.
The huge Labour majority in council has bred arrogance and complacency: councillors not replying to their constituents' concerns; housing damage left repaired; the council repeatedly siding with big developers over local residents; millions of the H&F pension fund still invested in Israeli and other arms companies. 
Thanks to our first-past-the-post voting system, Labour is likely to retain overall control in the Town Hall, but for the administration to be emboldened and held to account by Green Party councillors would be good for all of us (even Labour). We can win in Coningham: vote for hope, vote Green!

I’m fighting to keep our way of life in Rutland and Stamford, and the neighbouring areas I represent.

After fourteen years of Conservative government, everything I love about the place that raised me seems under threat.

Growing up outside of Billesdon gave me the chance to enjoy our beautiful scenery and untarnished towns. There’s something special about this area - whether it’s our sporting champions; our rich medieval and Georgian history; or our local traditions. I can’t remember an Easter Monday I haven’t spent at the Medbourne-Hallaton bottlekicking, and my times at local point to points are some of my favourite memories.

By volunteering I have helped the elderly and disabled, which has taught me the value of community; something that continues to be eroded by sweeping changes across this country. Beleaguered NHS waiting lists; millions out of work; small businesses struggling - our towns and villages become service and pub deserts, which erodes at community.

Whilst I worked in Brussels advocating for rural interests across Europe, I learned just how few voices there are making the case for common sense countryside policy. I had hoped that Brexit would help us break free from continental-style climate targets and metropolitan sensibilities. Back home, however, I’ve had to watch our fields plastered over to make way for expensive, destructive projects such as Mallard Pass Solar Farm.

A motorsport engineer by education, I am appalled to see Labour councils up and down this country enact ULEZ regulations and speed restrictions while elected Conservatives stand by idly. These are unscientific and if you have a basic understanding of how an engine works you will know that you use more fuel at lower gears with higher revs. Travel restrictions like these are a tax on working people and a grab at our freedom - I am determined to stop their creep into Stamford and Rutland.

Small businesses are the lifeblood of Rutland, and I plan to push for legislation that would

Vote Green for hope in a better future

- For a borough where residents are listened to

- Local policies for a greener, sustainable future

- Cleaner air and more affordable housing

- Repairs and improvements for residents, not demolitions for the benefit of billionaire investors

- An end to H&F's pension fund investing in the Israeli arms industry and all weapons factories

Looking forward to representing Fulham Reach as part of Hammersmith and Fulham council

Vote Green for hope in a better future

For a borough where residents are listened to

Local policies for a greener, sustainable future

Cleaner air and more affordable housing

Repairs and improvements for residents, not demolitions for the benefit of billionaire investors

An end to H&F's pension fund investing in the Israeli arms industry and all weapons factories

Vote Green for hope in a better future

For a borough where residents are listened to

Local policies for a greener, sustainable future

Cleaner air and more affordable housing

Repairs and improvements for residents, not demolitions for the benefit of billionaire investors

An end to H&F's pension fund investing in the Israeli arms industry and all weapons factories

Vote green for real change and to make hope normal again.

If elected, I pledge to;
 
-	Prioritise housing repairs and work to build more genuinely affordable and social homes
-	Listen to and work directly with residents to create new local plans 
-	Fight back against austerity and outsourcing 
-	Protect our parks and green spaces
-	Champion investment in green energy infrastructure such as solar panels, heat pumps, home retrofitting programmes and community energy initiatives.  
-	Protect free home care 
-	Bring our communities together around a shared agenda of hope

I have signed the following candidate pledges:

-The CPRE London-convened 10 Environmental Pledges
-The PSC Candidate Pledge for Palestine
-The Greens Organise Pledge to Oppose Austerity in Local Government"

We will work to build a fair and healthy community. Residents concerns will be our priority and we will push for democratic ways to involve residents in decisions that affect them. I am proud to live in this local area and want a better standard of living for all. We can help to achieve this through helping to make our borough energy secure with solar panels and heat pumps. This will help bring down our bills and make life more stable, help clean the air we breath and tackle the causes of the climate crisis.

We also aim to enrich our public spaces, the places where we connect: let's make clean green travel journeys the easiest choice to make, let's provide free-to-access activities like table tennis, chess, and outdoor gyms in our green spaces, let's have play streets.

We can get the basics right and aim for a positive vision of our local area. We look forward to serving our community.

As your councillor, I will focus on:

- Local green energy security through the expansion of solar, heat pumps, and retrofitting in the borough, as well as pioneering new approaches to community energy which allows H&F to reduce reliance on Fossil Fuels and imported energy.

- Create new and stronger powers to defend and protect tenants and renters. Increase the basic standard of housing decency, and combat spiralling rents by maximally disincentivising landlordism.

- Undertake holistic approaches to public safety. Less money poured into wasteful crime prevention, and more into social architecture, addressing the root causes of poverty and anti-social behaviour, and treating people as humans not potential criminals.

- Resist the council’s push to introduce AI surveillance drones.
Equality is not just a value; it's the essential first step to building a community strong enough to tackle our most urgent challenge: climate change.

Hi! My name is María, I’m 20 years old, and I have been living in the Lillie Ward for the past two years while studying for my Physics degree - essentially all of my young adult life so far. I’m eager to listen closely to the concerns of my neighbours and represent them as effectively as I can.

This area is full of energy and character. From the kind charity shop workers on North End Road to the children making friends over football in Normand Park, nothing motivates me more than helping to strengthen the strong sense of community that already exists in this ward.

I have spent much of my adult life feeling politically homeless, watching both traditional parties fail to deliver meaningful change. The Green Party is different, not just in its policies but in the way it conducts politics, with honesty, integrity and a genuine commitment to doing things differently.

Fulham is my home. My wife Juliette runs a business here, our boys Zac and Jago attended local schools and this community is where our family has put down deep roots. Every decision made about green spaces, housing, air quality and schools is personal to us.

By profession I am a banker with over two decades in financial services. I understand how economic decisions filter down to affect ordinary families and I bring that knowledge to how I think about council budgets and local investment. I also served as a governor at two local schools, which taught me more about the pressures on our public services than any policy paper ever could.

I am not a career politician. I come to this with fresh eyes and a genuine frustration at the status quo. The Green Party speaks to me because it connects the economic with the environmental and believes in real community participation rather than performative consultation.

Munster Ward deserves a councillor who will show up, listen and fight hard for residents. I bring professional expertise, community roots and no tribal loyalty, only a commitment to doing right by the people I would represent.

This year’s London Assembly elections are a real chance to change London for the better.

The Lib Dems are leading the fight to resolve the post-Grenfell cladding scandal, oppose the expansion of Heathrow, are pushing for a solution to the problem of Hammersmith Bridge after years of neglect by both Tory and Labour local administrations, and aim to tackle the air pollution that affects all of us, particularly our children.

London is a liberal city, particularly in West London where the Lib Dems came first in the Euro elections in 2019 by a long way. We also increased our share of the vote by 12% in the 2019 General Election while the share of both the Labour and Conservative parties fell.

Now more than ever we need new people with new ideas to tackle the severe challenges our great city faces, says Ted. We need the Lib Dems.

Ted has lived in Shepherds Bush and Fulham for over 30 years, sings in a local choir, serves on the board of a local charity, volunteers at a local food bank and tries to enjoy (when possible) Saturday morning park-runs.

Jeremy Maddocks, the prospective parliamentary candidate for the Brexit Party in Chelsea and Fulham, will contest the constituency in the next general election, standing against incumbent, Greg Hands.

Jeremy is a senior manager in an international technology security company and resident of Chelsea & Fulham for 25 years. He has been a long-standing opponent of Britain's membership of the EU.

Having worked in Russia and neighbouring countries for 15 years he has seen the impact of the dead hand of totalitarian, non-democratic systems on the governance of ordinary people.

He said: “Brexit offers a once in a lifetime opportunity for Britain to embrace international free trade across the globe. We will become wealthier, freer and self-governing, again. London is the greatest city on earth and we deserve to be liberated and open to the world.

"Knife crime, drugs and assault have reached unacceptable levels in our local community and we need real and proactive action from our elected representatives."

Jeremy has worked in international UK, US and European based technology companies for over 20 years. He was a Governor of Lady Margaret School, Fulham and is an active member and supporter of his local community.

In the early days of democracy in Eastern European countries he volunteered for international organisations to train young people in politics and communications. He has also set up an African Young Democrat Union to engender greater democracy and discourse in the region.

Jeremy studied at the Sorbonne in Paris, before attending Kings College London and then serving as a second lieutenant in the Royal Marines. He has been a director of youth for The Conservative Party.

As your local Fulham Candidate for the Palace and Hurlingham Ward Local Election on May 7th, I want to make sure that your voice is heard and your concerns are addressed by the Hammersmith & Fulham Council.

Aliya has lived in Parsons Green with her husband and two sons for over 10 years and in London for nearly 20 years. She runs her own educational software and App development company. Aliya is a Maths and Computer Science Teacher at Lady Margaret School, and previously taught at The Hurlingham Academy. She is actively involved in many local organisations including the H&F Volunteer Centre, the Society of Fulham Artists and Potters, and the Fulham Palace Trust. Aliya is passionate about our local parks and fixing broken/uneven pavements. She is an advocate for step-free access at Putney Bridge station. She wants residents to be able to enjoy our Borough safely and her priorities include cracking down on crime and anti-social behaviour; dealing with fly-tipping and graffiti; installing safe street crossings for children and the elderly, and improving air quality and traffic congestion.

There are three beautiful parks in my ward of Palace and Hurlingham and I am passionate about keeping them clean, well maintained and free of anti social behaviour. The council needs to fix broken and uneven pavements. I advocate for step free access at Putney Bridge tube station for the convenience of young families and the elderly. I want cleaner streets, more dog poo bins, removal of graffiti and flytipping efficiently. I enjoy walking and am concerned about the air quality and traffic congestion. The residents must be consulted properly before any Traffic Congestion Schemes are imposed in their neighbourhood which only moves the congestion and pollution to an adjacent street, while causing inconvenience to local residents, their visitors and contractors. I want the residents to enjoy safer lives and a cleaner environment in our wonderful borough with a crackdown on crime and ant

**About Matt**

Having moved to Fulham in 2024, it is a joy to be the Green Party candidate for Palace and Hurlingham, to offer to serve a community that has warmly welcomed my civil partner Peter (Vicar of All Saints Fulham),  our scruffy labradoodle, Finley, and myself, as we share in the life, the spaces and places, and the future of this ward.
Through my work for the NHS as it supports and cares for people experiencing homelessness, and in church and community work at St Andrew’s Fulham Fields, I see, every day, the diverse realities of life in our borough where health, housing, quality of life and opportunity can vary from street to street, neighbour to neighbour. 
On May 7th, we will all come to the ballot box across Palace and Hurlingham not just with our different political opinions or party affiliations but with the variety of experiences of life. We may prefer the Hurlingham Club to Craven Cottage, would rather a pint at The Eight Bells than a picnic in Bishop’s Park. We may know the comfort and security of a home where we have grown our families up in, or feel a real and present struggle of finding affordable and appropriate housing or the need for connection and community. When we vote we each carry all our needs alongside the pressures and impact that decisions made locally and nationally have on the people and places we love and care about.

**Why Matt?**

When voting Green this election, we bring our needs, our hopes and our dreams for our green spaces, our river, our air, our safety, our streets, our schools, our vital local services and networks to work in more sustainable, healthy, impactful and equitable ways. 
When voting Green this election, we are saying to those with power, that in a world where violent, divisive and othering rhetoric becomes ever louder and normalised, where the climate crisis is denied, and the value and worth of others is questioned, denied or actively destroyed  -we want to foster and create hopeful, just and compassionate

"Vote Green in Parsons Green and Sandford to:

- Hold Labour to account and take a stand against Reform and the far right 
- Take action for cleaner air and greener, healthier streets for all
- Protect our high streets and public spaces and make them thriving places for connection and community 
- Increase access to green space and nature - including by reducing the commercial use of our precious commons, parks and greens
- Push back against the use of extractive and unaccountable AI technologies and algorithms  
- Make housing affordable, safe and secure for all
- Oppose the defunding of our vital local services by central government 
- Ensure the council takes all the steps in its power to show solidarity with the Palestinian people and other victims of genocide, military occupation, ethnic cleansing and apartheid, and to avoid complicity in such forms of oppression and violence

I have signed the following candidate pledges:

- The CPRE London-convened 10 Environmental Pledges (https://www.cprelondon.org.uk/green-city/10-pledges/)

- The PSC Candidate Pledge for Palestine (https://palestinecampaign.org/council-candidate-pledge-for-palestine/)

- The Greens Organise Pledge to Oppose Austerity in Local Government (https://greensorganise.uk/pledge-oppose-austerity/) 
"

I am proud to call the beautiful Ravenscourt ward as the place me and my family call home. My day job is as a researcher the London School of Economics and Political Science, where I explore how city governments around Europe can try and do things differently to benefit citizens and the environment.

As your councillor, I would:
- tackle flytipping and littering along the river and in Ravenscourt Park
- keep our green spaces for public use, rather than for-profit events
- increase accessibility for people with pushchairs and wheelchairs
- urge the council to divest its pension investments benefitting which benefit from environmental degragdation, war and genocide around the world

A second generation Londoner, Henrietta went to school in Hammersmith and has lived in the borough for 30 years. After studying geology she worked as a computer programmer and business analyst for British Airways. She then became an opera singer and sang in the extra chorus at Covent Garden. She is now studying collaborative vocal improvisation and is a keen cyclist and Tango dancer.

I'm standing as a Green councillor because I believe that the residents deserve better. We deserve well funded local services and a clean environment. We deserve to be listened to and our needs and safety to be prioritised over the profits of billionaire investors.

The last few years, I've coordinated a mutual aid group and worked with charities to provide asylum seekers with a real community and essential services - healthy food, clothes, advice and exercise. Meanwhile, our government demonised immigrants and refugees instead of addressing the pressing concerns of our communities - rising rents, housing falling into disrepair, loss of public spaces and youth services.

As a councillor, I would work to amplify yours and your neighbours' voices. I want the council to work for the residents - the decision making process should be transparent and participatory.

My priorities are investing in affordable housing, youth services and community spaces, public transport as well as meaningful local climate action.

Ray Burnet is involved with many local charities, raising substantial funds for Maggie's at Charing Cross Hospital, local homeless, and the Shooting Star care for terminally ill children. Ray joined the Liberal Democrats after last year's referendum. "On local issues, it is clear to me that the ward is not being looked after by the Labour controlled Borough in terms of broken promises to its elderly. Labour have not replaced the Sands End Community Centre which was sold off to private developers. The nearest Police Station is now in Hammersmith, after Fulham Police Station has been handed over to a Free School! Free crèche facilities have been lost too. I will fight on behalf of the ward’s residents to reverse this disgraceful state of affairs and to make the area safer and more fun, building on initiatives like the new beach in place of the smelly, noisy car auction."

I’m Rafela and I’ve lived in Shepherd’s Bush since 1992. I love it here: the Market, the Theatre, the independent shops and restaurants, the incredible spirit and diversity of our community. But I’m not blind to our problems. For example, as a Geography teacher, I’m very aware of the impacts of climate change, globally and locally; I also teach about the deep and increasing inequality in UK society - and the reasons for it (spoiler: it’s not immigration!).  I have been very active volunteering in this community and I can’t wait to work with residents for positive changes in Shepherd’s Bush.

I am standing because I want Shepherd's Bush Green to be a place where everyone feels safe, supported and proud to live.
I have lived in this ward for years and I love it! Its energy, its community, and its wonderful mix of cultures. As Secretary of my Residents' Association, I know first-hand how frustrating it can be when basic problems go unfixed and residents feel unheard.
As a communications professional with experience in UK–EU policy work, I know how to cut through complexity and get things done. I will bring that same determination to this role.

My priorities:
- Women's safety: I want every resident, particularly women, to be able to move around this area with confidence at any time of day or night
- Safer streets: better lighting, regular walkabouts with police and council teams, and clear ways for residents to report issues and track progress
- Decent homes: quicker repairs, tougher action on damp and mould, and proper support for tenants and leaseholders

Labour has had twelve years to get this right. It is time for change.

I am standing because I believe the everyday basics that make life work, clean streets, responsive services, and a lively local high street, are worth fighting for.
I am a local resident and data consultant, and I bring a practical, problem-solving approach to everything I do. I am often out walking my dog around the ward and I see first-hand where things are falling short. Poorly maintained pavements, overflowing bins, and struggling independent businesses are not inevitable, they are the result of a council that has stopped paying attention.

My priorities:
- Cleaner streets: reliable waste management, rapid litter and graffiti removal, and a council that responds when residents raise concerns
- A thriving high street: practical support for independent businesses on Uxbridge Road and Goldhawk Road, keeping the area lively and welcoming
- Accountable council services: transparent performance, faster repairs, and councillors who actually show up and fight for residents

Shepherd's Bush Green deserves councillors who are present, practical and on your side.

Chris Vinante (Christian Vinante Giovannini), is a Social/Political campaigns Consultant. Visiting Research Fellow in Social Media at the Department of Life and Medical Sciences at the University of Hertfordshire. Until May 2021 he was Deputy Field Director – Consultant for the Conservatives’ London Mayoral Campaign, and previously as a Senior Advisor (Outreach) from December 2019. Now is a free lance Social Media advisor for the Conservative Party London Area. Founder and Chairman of the Westminster Italian Conservatives Group. Campaign Director for the Foreign and Commonwealth Council. In 2018 he was the Conservatives’ Deputy Chair and Representative Candidate for the London Brentford Ward in the local elections. In 2018 he was appointed deputy director of the British/Chinese Youth Federation until January 2020. Committee Member at the Westminster Arab - Middle East & North Africa Group.  From January 2019 Honorary President of The Parliamentary Society of Arts Fashion and Sports, of which he is a co-founder. Co-Founder and Executive Director of The British-Italian Conservatives Group from Jan 2019 until Nov 2020. In 2018 a University of Arts London group of students did an exam video documentary with Chris’ career as its subject. Chris is also a singer/events organizer, former director of The Green Room Networking Club. As a singer he has performed at the Palace of Westminster, Harrods, Hotel Cafe Royal, Royal Horseguards Hotel Whitehall, Millennium Hotel Mayfair and Knightsbridge, Chinawhite Club, Kensington Town Hall, Inception Clubs and Westfields. Vinante has also performed for various Scotland Yard staff Parties, for the City Police HQ and many top bank branches such as NatWest, Barclays and Lloyds TSB. He has sung at events attended by Leona Lewis, Deputy PM Dominic Raab MP, Princess Marie-Esméralda of Belgium, The Princes Katerina of Yugoslavia and many UK Gov. ministers. Chris co-organised a birthday party event in honour of, and with the participation o

The voices of people in our community matter.

Social tenants' housing concerns around damp, mould, and repairs are frequently ignored. And communication with residents is often poor.

By involving locals in decision-making and working together, we can make change happen.

Greens will also divest the millions of H&F pension fund currently invested in the Gaza genocide.

Vote Green and let's make hope normal again.

Councillor Asif Siddique:

By Profession I am a Leading Solicitor, also Barrister by education and Councillor by passion.

I have lived in London over 20 years and made Hammersmith and Fulham my home with my wife, daughter and baby son. I am actively involved in helping the local community, small businesses and previously running one of the Neighbourhood watch schemes within the area. By profession I am a practising Leading Solicitor, also a Barrister by education in London and a Human rights activist with extensive experience in Immigration, Asylum, EU law, Family law and Housing. I am working with the collaboration of numerous mainstream charities, providing a FREE WILL writing service to all senior citizens in London. I speak multiple languages.

I am determined to keep our streets and parks safe and clean & green for all and improve cohesion within our multicultural community. I fought hard for the EU Remain campaign and Saving Charing Cross Hospital having 6 family members who are doctors living within the LBHF Borough and ardently believes in improving education and career skills for students which has inspired me to become a Labour Councillor candidate for a third time for the Local Government in London.

Since being elected as a Wendell Park Ward Councillor in 2022, I have worked fervently with the local residents leading on multiple projects including supporting the local residents in Wendell Park ward in their plight to stop the erection of a 20m tall 5G mast in our Ward. I facilitated two zebra crossings close to Wendell Park school for the local residents, improving the safety of our children and families/residents in the area. I am actively leading and supporting the residents of Pocklington Lodge (TPT) to retain their accommodation especially for the blind and partially sighted people.

I am involved in walking with the LET officers in our Wendell Park ward to promote safety and prevent anti-social behaviour and crime in our Ward. I was involved in the

I’m standing in Wendell Park because I believe in a fairer, greener future for Hammersmith and Fulham. I want to champion causes with the community at their core, and will fight for the environment, affordable housing, and to protect Shepherd’s Bush Market. Having spent the last decade of my career campaigning for assisted dying, I know how to make change happen, and I hope to bring that change to Hammersmith and Fulham.

Harry has been a councillor since 2006 and is the Conservative spokesman on housing.
He is married with three daughters and is a journalist.
He has served as a school governor and a housing association board member.

I am standing to help offer a fairer, more sustainable future. In a time of increasing fear and division, the Green Party offers an agenda of hope. I stand for:

- More affordable housing 
- Living wages
- Support for the most vulnerable in society 
- Focus on Green Growth
- Challenging the overreach of big tech companies in our creative communities and in our private lives.

I am a scientist and science communicator by training who has contributed in the fields of genetics, neuroscience and AI. I also work as a therapist, and volunteer for literacy programmed in local schools.

I have signed the following candidate pledges: 
- The CPRE London-convened 10 Environmental Pledges 
- The PSC Candidate Pledge for Palestine 
- The Greens Organise Pledge to Oppose Austerity in Local Government

I have lived in Shepherd’s Bush since I was born, I work a retail job, and in my free time I am a community organiser. During the pandemic I helped renters know their rights and stand up to bad landlords through community action. Recently, I have campaigned to protect Shepherd’s Bush Market and get the council to divest its pension fund from companies complicit in Israel’s war crimes.

Like many of you, I always used to vote Labour – but Labour no longer speak for us. The Green Party are different, we stand up for high-quality, well-maintained housing; we trust communities to make decisions; and we unequivocally stand against genocide.

Together with Emma and Zayna these are our top priorities:

-Better housing repairs and new council housing for local people
-Keep Shepherd’s Bush Market affordable
-Improve and care for Uxbridge Road
-Stop illegal parking and littering
-Tackle fly tipping & anti-social behaviour
-Be a voice for residents
-Scrutinise the Labour-dominated council

I am dedicated to working towards ensuring habitable and well-maintained housing across the ward, as Hammersmith & Fulham, including White City, has one of the worst track records for the maintenance of council housing in the country.

Beyond that, I am dedicated to providing a voice for everyone in the ward and finding solutions for problems that work for directly for residents.
